content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Fixed `run stop` forced-stop behavior so Unix stale-backend cleanup now still SIGKILLs orphaned backend process groups by PGID after the recorded leader exits, waits on real surviving group members instead of only the leader PID, and ignores zombie-only remnants during verification. Refactored the CLI stop path so a TERM-ignoring orchestrator is always SIGKILLed and observed dead before backend cleanup runs, then finalizes the interrupted snapshot and `run.pid` handoff even if backend cleanup later fails. Updated CLI coverage to assert orphaned backend-group children are terminated and that forced-stop snapshot reconciliation still completes when backend cleanup reports an error.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the pending amendments in `src/cli/run.rs` and the affected CLI regressions to confirm where orphaned backend PGID cleanup and forced-stop ordering were still wrong.
2. [Completed] Patch Unix stale-backend cleanup so leaderless backend groups are still SIGKILLed by PGID, and so post-kill verification tracks live process-group members instead of only the recorded leader PID.
3. [Completed] Refactor forced `run stop` execution so SIGKILL of the orchestrator happens before backend cleanup, run-state reconciliation no longer re-attempts backend cleanup in that path, and cleanup failures are surfaced only after durable stop finalization.
4. [Completed] Replace the regression that locked in orphan survival, add a regression for stop finalization under backend-cleanup failure, then run targeted tests plus the full acceptance-command suite.

## Validation Evidence

- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test run_stop_sigkill_terminates_orphaned_backend_process_group_child -- --nocapture`.
- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test run_stop_sigkill_finalizes_snapshot_even_when_backend_cleanup_fails -- --nocapture`.
- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test run_stop_recovers_stale_running_and_kills_persisted_backend_process_group -- --nocapture`.
- Full suite passed: `cargo test`.
- Lint suite passed with pre-existing warnings only: `cargo clippy`.
- Formatting check passed: `cargo fmt --check`.
- Backlog export check passed: `br sync --flush-only` reported `Nothing to export (no dirty issues)`.
- Backlog lint was run and reported only unrelated pre-existing template warnings: `br lint`.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy` passes with pre-existing warnings outside this change set (for example `dead_code`, `ptr_arg`, `large_enum_variant`, and other existing style warnings).
- `br lint` still reports unrelated pre-existing template warnings on other beads (`ralph-burning-rlm.1`, `.2`, `.3`, `.5`, and several others).
- The Unix process-group path was validated on this Linux host; the shared Unix implementation for non-Linux platforms was not exercised end-to-end in this pass.
